howItWorks.intro: This moving cost calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: DIY saves 50-70% but requires more time and effort
howItWorks.outro: Real estate values and regulations vary significantly by location. Work with a local agent or attorney.

faq.question: How much does it cost to move?
faq.answer: Local: $400-2,500. Long distance (state to state): $2,000-7,500. Cross country: $4,000-12,000+. DIY truck rental: 50-70% less. Costs vary by home size, distance, time of year, and services needed.

faq.question: When is the cheapest time to move?
faq.answer: September through April (off-season) is 20-30% cheaper than peak season (May-August). Mid-month and mid-week moves are cheaper than month-end and weekends. Avoid holidays and the first/last days of the month.

faq.question: What hidden moving costs should I budget for?
faq.answer: Common extras: packing materials ($100-300), insurance/valuation ($50-200), long carry fees ($75-150), stair fees ($75/flight), storage ($100-300/month), tip for movers (15-20%), and cleaning old/new home ($100-300).

howWeCalculate.intro: This moving cost calculator uses established formulas and current data to provide accurate estimates.
steps: Enter your specific values into the calculator fields above
Our algorithm applies the relevant formulas using your inputs
Results are calculated instantly in your browser — nothing is sent to a server
Review the detailed breakdown to understand how each factor affects your result
howWeCalculate.disclaimer: These calculations are estimates based on standard formulas. For critical decisions, always consult a qualified professional.

whenToUse.intro: This moving cost calculator is designed for anyone who needs quick, reliable estimates.
scenarios: When you need a quick estimate before committing to a purchase or project
When comparing different options or scenarios side by side
When planning a budget and need to understand potential costs
When you want to verify a quote or estimate you've received
When teaching or learning about the concepts behind these calculations
